weeklyStories.headline: The government wants to preview AI models before you can.
weeklyStories.body: Trump signed an executive order giving federal agencies a 30-day sneak peek at powerful new AI models before they go public. It's voluntary — no one's getting blocked — but the vibe is clear: the bouncer wants to see the guest list before the doors open.

weeklyStories.headline: Anthropic's AI is now training itself. Yes, like that.
weeklyStories.body: Anthropic announced their AI systems are writing code for other AI systems — a recursive improvement milestone. Think: the intern who got good enough to onboard next year's interns without supervision. Development velocity just got a booster shot.

weeklyStories.headline: OpenAI shipped a product without writing a single line of code.
weeklyStories.body: Their team built an entire product using only AI coding agents. The humans didn't code — they designed the environment the AI works in. It's called harness engineering. The model is talented, but the real magic is how you set up the room. Cher didn't pick outfits randomly. She built the closet computer first.

weeklyStories.headline: An AI tax system processed 7,000 returns at 97% accuracy.
weeklyStories.body: OpenAI and Thrive launched a system that learns from accountant corrections in real time — like an associate who actually remembers the feedback. It saved a third of prep time per return. The future of professional work isn't AI replacing you. It's AI that gets better because of you.

dailyHeadlines.headline: Google's Gemini 2.5 is now the default model everywhere.
dailyHeadlines.body: Every Google product quietly switched to Gemini 2.5 this week. Search, Docs, Gmail, Workspace — all running the same model. The AI arms race just became the default-apps race.

dailyHeadlines.headline: Apple announced 'Apple Intelligence' is finally shipping in full.
dailyHeadlines.body: After months of staggered rollouts, all the promised features are live in iOS 19 beta. Siri can finally do things people have been asking for since 2016.

dailyHeadlines.headline: Microsoft is charging $30/month for Copilot Pro. Corporates are balking.
dailyHeadlines.body: The per-seat cost of enterprise AI tools is becoming the budget conversation nobody prepared for. One CFO called it 'the new Salesforce creep.'
